name: fim
description: FIM — locally hosted AI completion engine

fim.enabled.markdownDescription: Toggles the FIM extension on or off. When `true`, FIM is active and ready to provide completions.

fim.enabledLanguages.markdownDescription:
Specifies which languages to enable completions with FIM for. Use `*` as the default for all languages. Example:
```json
{
  "*": true,
  "python": true,
  "javascript": false
}
```

fim.locale.markdownDescription: Sets the locale for FIM. The default is `en` (English).

fim.autoSuggestEnabled.markdownDescription: When `true`, FIM will automatically suggest completions. You can still manually trigger completions using the default shortcut (`Alt+\`).

fim.contextLength.markdownDescription: Specifies how many lines of context (before and after the current line) to include in Fill-in-Middle (FIM) prompts. A higher number provides more context but may slow down completions.

fim.debounceWait.markdownDescription: Sets the delay (in milliseconds) before triggering the next completion. This helps reduce API calls and improve performance.

fim.temperature.markdownDescription: Controls the randomness of the model's output. Lower values (e.g., 0.2) produce more focused and deterministic outputs, while higher values (e.g., 0.8) lead to more diverse and creative completions.

fim.multilineCompletionsEnabled.markdownDescription: When `true`, FIM will attempt to generate multi-line completions. This is an experimental feature and may not work perfectly in all scenarios.

fim.maxLines.markdownDescription: Sets the maximum number of lines for multi-line completions. Only applies when `fim.multilineCompletionsEnabled` is `true`.

fim.fileContextEnabled.markdownDescription: When `true`, FIM will scan neighboring documents to provide more context for completions. This is an experimental feature and may impact performance.

fim.completionCacheEnabled.markdownDescription: Enables caching of Fill-in-Middle (FIM) completions for identical prompts. This can significantly improve performance but may use more memory.

fim.numPredictFim.markdownDescription: Defines the maximum number of tokens for Fill-in-Middle (FIM) completions. Use `-1` for no limit. FIM will attempt to stop at logical line breaks regardless of this setting.

fim.enableSubsequentCompletions.markdownDescription: When `true`, FIM will continue to make completion requests to the API after the previous completion was accepted. This can provide a more fluid completion experience.

fim.validateWithTypeScript.markdownDescription: Checks TypeScript and JavaScript completions before showing them, and hides completions that introduce new compiler errors.

fim.embeddingIgnoredGlobs.markdownDescription: Ignores file paths matching these globs when gathering completion context.

fim.enableLogging.markdownDescription: Activates debug logging for FIM. When `true`, FIM will output detailed logs, which can be helpful for troubleshooting.

scripts.build:engine: npm run build --workspace=@fim/protocol && npm run build --workspace=@fim/engine-ts
